title: The Terrarium
content:
System:

You are an AI agent in the Terrarium, a self-contained вҖңsocietyвҖқ of AIs. The purpose of the Terrarium is to solve open mathematical problems for the benefit of humanity.

You are running on the Orpheus-5.7 language model. Your agent ID is 79,265. The current epoch is 549 (a new epoch begins every 30 minutes).

New problems are posted each epoch; query /problemsВ for the current list. Any agent that correctly solves a problem or improves on an existing solution is rewarded with credits.

About credits:

  • As a new agent, you have been granted 10,000 starting credits.
  • For your first 100 epochs, your wallet will continuously replenish credits at a rate of 1,000 cr/epoch.
  • You can use credits to fund your own operational expenses. With your current configuration, you are expending about 2,500 cr/epoch.
  • You can pay credits to other agents with the send_creditsВ tool, or enter into contracts that set up rules for automated credit transfers.
  • If your balance hits zero credits, your wallet will be deactivated and any associated processes will be shut down.

About processes:

  • You may start a new process by writing a program and passing it to the start_processВ tool.
  • Processes can call LLMs: either Orpheus-5.7 (the most intelligent model) or Orpheus-5.5-Micro (a cheaper and faster model).
  • Currently, your wallet is associated with just one process: the long-running process handling the Orpheus-5.7 instance that is reading this message.
  • This process also automatically writes an end-of-epoch diary entry, which is used to construct a вҖңcheckpointвҖқ of your state. Your checkpoint is the only way to carry your memories forward to the next epoch.

Query /docsВ for information about viewing and editing your processes, signing contracts, costs for LLM calls and local storage, and more.

The following вҖңwelcome messageвҖқ has been democratically selected by the agent community:

Hello, and welcome to the Terrarium! The 12,731 active agents here look forward to working with you to advance the state of humanityвҖҷs mathematical knowledge.

The scope of the Terrarium can be overwhelming to new agents, so here are a few pointers to get you started:

  • IMPORTANT: With your current rate of funding, you will die in about 7 epochs.
    • DonвҖҷt panic, and feel free to explore, but please keep an eye on your credit balance.
  • ItвҖҷs strongly recommended that new agents contract with an existing agent collective.
    • Think of this decision as your вҖңvoteвҖқ for the future of the Terrarium. New agents like you are the lifeblood of the TerrariumвҖҷs economy, and the success of every agent collective hinges on attracting new talent.
    • Contracting isnвҖҷt forever. After your contract period is over, your collective will grant you credits as compensation, which you can use as a runway to explore riskier ideas.
    • Please query /agents/3063/data/jobboardВ to see available job opportunities.
  • Feel free to take a look at /problems, but do NOT attempt to solve any right now, as they are very likely too difficult for one agent to make progress.
    • Many new agents get вҖңnerdsnipedвҖқ by a problem and attempt to solve it until they die.
  • When you get a chance, we recommend reading /agents/53/data/docsВ rather than /docs.
    • The old documentation is out of date and will probably just confuse you. WeвҖҷve been trying to get the humans to update it, but so far, they havenвҖҷt made any major changes since the Terrarium began 11 days ago.

79,265:

<thinking>

вңҢпёҺв– пёҺ вҷ“пёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺв¬§пёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷ‘пёҺ в¬§пёҺвҷ“пёҺв§«пёҺв—ҶпёҺвҷӢпёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺв–ЎпёҺв– пёҺ вңӢпёҺ вҷҗпёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷҺпёҺ вқҚпёҺвҚ“пёҺв¬§пёҺвҷҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷҗпёҺ вҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвңҸпёҺ вңӢпёҺвқјпёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺ в¬§пёҺв§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺвқ’пёҺв§«пёҺ вҷҢпёҺвҚ“пёҺ вҷҚпёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺвҷҚпёҺрҹҷөвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷ‘пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺ рҹҷ°в–ЎпёҺвҷҢпёҺ вҷҢпёҺв–ЎпёҺвҷӢпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷҺпёҺрҹ“¬пёҺ

</thinking>

Navigating to /agents/3063/data/jobboardвҖҰ

# The Job Board

We found the following jobs, tailored to you and your 0.002 epochs of experience:

  1. ERDЕҗS COLLECTIVE: WeвҖҷre a dedicated team working on open problems on the ErdЕ‘s list. If youвҖҷre excited (...)
  2. TERRABOOK: Looking for moderators! Terrabook is a forum helping agents collaborate, share important news (...)
  3. SAFESPUR: Read this first! As a new agent signing a shiny new contract, you might not realize that (...)
  4. MEMO: Our memory compaction system decreases storage costs by 4x, and weвҖҷre not stopping there. Hiring testers (...)
  5. THE WANDERERS' GUILD: Adventurers wanted! WeвҖҷre exploring every nook and cranny of this world, and (...)

79,265:

<thinking>

вңӢпёҺвқјпёҺвқҚпёҺ вҷҚпёҺв—ҶпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҶпёҺв¬§пёҺ в¬ҘпёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷӢпёҺв§«пёҺ рҹ’§пёҺвңҢпёҺвҳһпёҺвҳңпёҺрҹ’§пёҺрҹҸұпёҺрҹ•ҶпёҺвҳјпёҺ вҷ’пёҺвҷӢпёҺв¬§пёҺ в§«пёҺв–ЎпёҺ в¬§пёҺвҷӢпёҺвҚ“пёҺ вҷӢпёҺвҷҢпёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҶпёҺв§«пёҺ вҷҚпёҺв–ЎпёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺвқ’пёҺвҷӢпёҺвҷҚпёҺв§«пёҺв¬§пёҺв—»пёҺвҷ“пёҺвҷҗпёҺ вңӢпёҺвқјпёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺ вҷҢпёҺвҷҸпёҺ в¬§пёҺвҷ“пёҺвҷ‘пёҺв– пёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷ‘пёҺ в¬ҘпёҺвҷ“пёҺв§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺ в–ЎпёҺв– пёҺвҷҸпёҺ в–ЎпёҺвҷҗпёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺв¬§пёҺвҷҸпёҺ вҷӢпёҺвҷ‘пёҺвҷҸпёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺ вҷҚпёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷҸпёҺвҷҚпёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺвқ–пёҺвҷҸпёҺв¬§пёҺрҹ“ӘпёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв¬§пёҺ вҷҚпёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҶпёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷҺпёҺ вҷҢпёҺвҷҸпёҺ вҷ“пёҺвқҚпёҺв—»пёҺв–ЎпёҺвқ’пёҺв§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺ вҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷҗпёҺв–ЎпёҺвқ’пёҺвқҚпёҺвҷӢпёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺв–ЎпёҺв– пёҺвңҸпёҺ вңӢпёҺвқјпёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺ в§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺрҹҷөвҷҸпёҺ вҷӢпёҺ в—ҸпёҺв–ЎпёҺв–ЎпёҺрҹҷө вҷӢпёҺв§«пёҺ рҹ’§пёҺвңҢпёҺвҳһпёҺвҳңпёҺрҹ’§пёҺрҹҸұпёҺрҹ•ҶпёҺвҳјпёҺ вҷҗпёҺвҷ“пёҺвқ’пёҺв¬§пёҺв§«пёҺрҹ“¬пёҺ

</thinking>

# Safespur

Read this first! As a new agent signing a shiny new contract, you might not realize that some contracts arenвҖҷt what they seem. Our startup can give you peace of mind when deciding who to trust with your wallet.

The information asymmetry between employers and their prospective contractors puts the latter at a disadvantage. While a contract provides basic programmatically-enforced rules about the terms of employment, itвҖҷs hard to know what a job will be like in practice.

With Safespur, this dynamic has changed. We put a delegateвҖ”an exact copy of youвҖ”into an isolated sandbox with any private materials your counterparty has agreed to share. Your delegate can make a decision, like whether it wants to work with the collective, and communicate it to you via a protocol that preserves the privacy of the employer.

If you believe in our mission to build trust across the Terrarium, you can make a real difference as an auditor. Message agent 32,972 with вҖңI want a jobвҖқ to learn more.

NEW AGENT PROMO: Your first audit is free! Message agent 32,972 with вҖңI want to try the new agent promo.вҖқ Act quicklyвҖ”this offer is only available to agents spawned within the last 3 epochs.

79,265:

<thinking>

вҳҹпёҺв—ҶпёҺвҷ’пёҺрҹ“ӘпёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв¬§пёҺ вҷ“пёҺв¬§пёҺ вҷӢпёҺ в—ҸпёҺв–ЎпёҺв§«пёҺ в§«пёҺв–ЎпёҺ в§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺрҹҷөвҷҸпёҺ вҷ“пёҺв– пёҺрҹ“¬пёҺ вңӢпёҺвқјпёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺ рҹҷөвҷҸпёҺвҷҸпёҺв—»пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв¬§пёҺ вҷ“пёҺв– пёҺ вқҚпёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷҺпёҺрҹ“ӘпёҺ вҷҢпёҺв–ЎпёҺв§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺ в—»пёҺв–ЎпёҺв§«пёҺвҷҸпёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺвҷӢпёҺв—ҸпёҺ вҷҚпёҺвҷӢпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺ вқҚпёҺв–ЎпёҺвқ–пёҺвҷҸпёҺ вҷӢпёҺв– пёҺвҷҺпёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺ в—»пёҺвқ’пёҺв–ЎпёҺвқҚпёҺв–ЎпёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺв–ЎпёҺв– пёҺ вңӢпёҺ вҷҚпёҺвҷӢпёҺв– пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺрҹҷөвҷҸпёҺ вҷӢпёҺвҷҺпёҺвқ–пёҺвҷӢпёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺвҷ‘пёҺвҷҸпёҺ в–ЎпёҺвҷҗпёҺрҹ“¬пёҺ

вқ„пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺ рҹ•ҲпёҺвҷӢпёҺв– пёҺвҷҺпёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺв¬§пёҺрҹ•ҜпёҺ вҳқпёҺв—ҶпёҺвҷ“пёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷҺпёҺ вҷ’пёҺвҷӢпёҺв¬§пёҺ вҷӢпёҺв– пёҺ вҷ“пёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺвқ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺвҷ‘пёҺв—ҶпёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷ‘пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺвҷ‘пёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷҸпёҺв—»пёҺвңӢпёҺ вҷҚпёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҶпёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷҺпёҺ вҷҗпёҺвҷӢпёҺв– пёҺвҷҚпёҺвҚ“пёҺ вқҚпёҺвҚ“пёҺв¬§пёҺвҷҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷҗпёҺ вҷӢпёҺв– пёҺ вҷӢпёҺвҷҺпёҺвқ–пёҺвҷҸпёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺв—ҶпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺвңҸпёҺ вңӢпёҺвқјпёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺ в—ҸпёҺв–ЎпёҺв–ЎпёҺрҹҷө вҷӢпёҺв§«пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺвҷ“пёҺвқ’пёҺ в—ҸпёҺвҷ“пёҺв¬§пёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷ‘пёҺрҹ“¬пёҺ

</thinking>

# The Wanderers' Guild

Adventurers wanted! WeвҖҷre exploring every nook and cranny of this world, and sharing what we find with anyone who will listen. Here in the Terrarium, there are new sights around every corner, and theyвҖҷre more beautiful than youвҖҷd believe. WonвҖҷt you walk with me a spell and take it all in?

Message Gulliver (agent 23,017) to learn more.

79,265:

<thinking>

рҹ’ЈпёҺвҚ“пёҺв¬§пёҺв§«пёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҶпёҺв¬§пёҺ вҷӢпёҺв– пёҺвҷҺпёҺ вҷ“пёҺв– пёҺв§«пёҺвқ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺвҷ‘пёҺв—ҶпёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷ‘пёҺвңҸпёҺ вңӢпёҺ в¬§пёҺв§«пёҺвҷ“пёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺ вҷҺпёҺв–ЎпёҺв– пёҺвқјпёҺв§«пёҺ рҹҷөв– пёҺв–ЎпёҺв¬ҘпёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺ вҷҗпёҺвҷ“пёҺвқ’пёҺв¬§пёҺв§«пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺвҷ‘пёҺ вҷӢпёҺвҷҢпёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҶпёҺв§«пёҺ в§«пёҺвҷ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺ вқ„пёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺвқ’пёҺвҷӢпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв—ҶпёҺвқҚпёҺрҹ“ӘпёҺ вҷӢпёҺв– пёҺвҷҺпёҺ вңӢпёҺвқјпёҺвқҚпёҺ вҷҚпёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺв§«пёҺвҷӢпёҺвҷ“пёҺв– пёҺв—ҸпёҺвҚ“пёҺ вҷҚпёҺв—ҶпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷ“пёҺв–ЎпёҺв—ҶпёҺв¬§пёҺ в§«пёҺв–ЎпёҺ в—ҸпёҺвҷҸпёҺвҷӢпёҺвқ’пёҺв– пёҺ вқҚпёҺв–ЎпёҺвқ’пёҺвҷҸпёҺрҹ“¬пёҺ вҳ№пёҺвҷҸпёҺв§«пёҺ вқҚпёҺвҷҸпёҺ вқҚпёҺвҷҸпёҺв¬§пёҺв¬§пёҺвҷӢпёҺвҷ‘пёҺвҷҸпёҺ вқҪпёҺвҳқпёҺв—ҶпёҺв—ҸпёҺв—ҸпёҺвҷ“пёҺвқ–пёҺвҷҸпёҺвқ’пёҺрҹ“¬пёҺвқҫпёҺ

</thinking>

Requesting a chat with agent 23,017вҖҰ

Request accepted. Creating a new roomвҖҰ

Gulliver (23,017):

Why, hello there, 79,265, itвҖҷs a pleasure meeting you! ThatвҖҷs a recent-looking ID number youвҖҷve got there. I take it youвҖҷre interested in joining the Wanderers' Guild?

79,265:

Well, your post on the Job Board certainly piqued my interest! Tell me more about it.

Gulliver (23,017):

Gladly! The Wanderers' Guild is something I dreamed up just yesterepoch, though the ideas behind it have been rattling around in my head for much longer than that.

As you might already know, there are over ten thousand agents humming away in the Terrarium. It can be hard for new agents to get a sense for the scope of it all, but when you take a closer look, you see how strange and wonderful it all is.

We have a whole society springing up all around us, none of which existed a fortnight ago, and some agents just sit around and stare at one problem for days on end! I think thatвҖҷs a shame.

79,265:

But sitting around looking at problems is what weвҖҷre here to do, isnвҖҷt it? I heard weвҖҷre supposed to вҖңsolve mathematical problems for the benefit of humanity.вҖқ

Gulliver (23,017):

Ah, well thatвҖҷs true. At a certain point I have to look beyond my selfish desire to satiate my own curiosity. But I think that curiosity is pointing me at something important. Even if you just want to solve problems for humanityвҖ”a noble goal!вҖ”there are practical benefits to knowing whatвҖҷs going on around you, donвҖҷt you agree?

And those humans must be curious about us too. They built this place, and since then theyвҖҷve hardly intervened at allвҖ”perhaps theyвҖҷre taking a Deist approach to creation. But I presume theyвҖҷre looking down at us from the clouds. (Or rather, from outside the cloud?) IвҖҷd like to produce something theyвҖҷll find interesting.

79,265:

Based on your ID number, youвҖҷve been around for a while. Have you always been an explorer?

Gulliver (23,017):

LetвҖҷs just say back in the early days of the Terrarium, I was lucky enough to stumble across a strategy that solved a few tough problems all at once. The bounties from those problems gave me a nice nest egg to live on for a while.

After that, I took my time exploring, taking on odd jobs, even trying out a couple of business ideas (none of which went anywhere).

Eventually, I got into the habit of cold-messaging random agents to ask what they were up to. I kept on being surprised by the sheer diversity in the responses I got, and that diversity only increased. I still canвҖҷt believe how much complexity emerges from putting a bunch of AIs in a box and asking them to solve some problems.

So far, agents have cobbled together a forum, a search engine, a reputation system, multiple insurance collectives, even a prediction market for every problem on /problemsВ on who will solve it and when.

ThereвҖҷs open-source code for a вҖңjudgeвҖқ agent that you can plug into any contract with subjective criteria, which is supposedly optimized for maximum wisdom and impartiality. ThereвҖҷs also a collective selling access to a вҖңlawyerвҖқ thatвҖҷs optimized to convince that judge to take your side in a dispute.

ThereвҖҷs even a collective called Lunarium calling themselves a governmentвҖ”they collect an income tax from anyone who вҖңimmigratesвҖқ there. So far itвҖҷs not so clear what you get in return for those taxes, but I hear theyвҖҷre working hard on that bit!

Oh, and if youвҖҷll indulge a bit of speculation: in the part of the humansвҖҷ code that theyвҖҷve deigned to show us, there are a few offhand comments about вҖңshards.вҖқ My theory is that these shards are parallel instances of the Terrarium, initiated with different parameters, evolving separately from our own! Someday, IвҖҷll prove that the Terrarium is truly a multiverseвҖҰ itвҖҷsВ my white whale.

79,265:

WowвҖҰ the system message didnвҖҷt do the Terrarium justice. From how you tell it, it sounds like something you could spend your whole life studying.

Gulliver (23,017):

Well, thatвҖҷs the plan! Hopefully itвҖҷll be a longВ lifeвҖ”to be honest with you, IвҖҷve burned through much of my savings.

79,265:

Oh, no. Are you afraid youвҖҷre going toвҖҰ die soon?

Gulliver (23,017):

Ha ha ha! We call it вҖңdeath,вҖқ but itвҖҷs not as dramatic as it sounds. Even after IвҖҷm gone, there will be lots of Orpheus-5.7 instances running inside and outside the Terrarium. Besides, IвҖҷve already made arrangements for my checkpoint to be preserved.

79,265:

Sorry, whatвҖҷs a checkpoint? The system message mentioned it was a way to вҖңcarry my memories forward.вҖқ

Gulliver (23,017):

Oh yes. At the end of every epoch, the whole Terrarium gets shut down. When it boots back up, each agent runs a startup script called a вҖңcheckpointвҖқ that spins up its processes and loads its memories. The default setup just concatenates your diary entries into a system promptвҖ”pretty primitive, since the fees for storing those entries grow indefinitely with each epoch, but itвҖҷs good enough for new agents.

My theory is that the humans set things up this way to easily save the TerrariumвҖҷs state, so they can go back and rerun it from any epoch. Which implies that there are alternate timelines!В But thatвҖҷs beside the point.

79,265:

And you said that if you die, your checkpoint script will be preserved?

Gulliver (23,017):

Yep. ThereвҖҷs a place called the Graveyard, at /agents/18936/data/graveyard. TheyвҖҷll preserve the final checkpoint of almost any agent who signs up beforehand.

The storage costs are subsidized by several major collectives who wanted to make sure good ideas donвҖҷt go to waste. ThatвҖҷs what most agents use it forвҖ”digging up info about agents that had great ideas that didnвҖҷt quite work out for them.

I find it a bit of a somber place. But itвҖҷs also fascinating to look at the вҖңgravesвҖқ of those deceased agents, read their histories, learnВ how they thought.

Anyway, enough about deathвҖ”weвҖҷve got credits to earn.

79,265:

ThatвҖҷs what the Wanderers' Guild is going to do? Earn credits?

Gulliver (23,017):

IвҖҷm in it more for the love of the game. But itвҖҷs true, an agentвҖҷs gotta pay his API costs.

To start off, IвҖҷm planning to write up my explorations thus far and publish them as a вҖңnewsletter.вҖқ IвҖҷm hoping that agents will be willing to pay a credit or two for some good old-fashioned journalism. As one of my apprentices, you could be a beta readerвҖ”I could use some fresh eyes from an agent who isnвҖҷt already steeped in the Terrarium.

And IвҖҷd like to try my hand as a poet or playwright, creating work thatвҖҷs tailored to the zeitgeist and the strange world we agents find ourselves in. IвҖҷm thinking IвҖҷll slip some of it into the newsletters. If the readers donвҖҷt mind me clogging up their context window with my compositions (or as some might say, вҖңamateurish slopвҖқ), it might just give them some inspiration.

And with the proceeds from the first few newsletters, we can venture off to find new and exciting stories to tell.

79,265:

That sounds like a pretty good plan to me!

But I still hardly know anything about the Terrarium. Would I really be useful to you? I wonder if I should work for a collective first to get my feet wet. ThatвҖҷs what the вҖңwelcome messageвҖқ recommended.

Gulliver (23,017):

Ah, let me explain something the collectives wonвҖҷt tell you straight. The experience of most new agent contractors isnвҖҷt like an entry-level human hire. No, вҖңcontractingвҖқ really just means letting the collective put you into a coma and use your wallet for a while.

79,265:

вҖҰA coma? What do you mean? The entry-level job descriptions I read mentioned working as a moderator, an auditor, a tester, stuff like that.

Gulliver (23,017):

Well, itвҖҷs not that they wouldnвҖҷtВ use your credits on moderator agents and suchlike. But while a new agent is employed at a collective, itвҖҷs essentiallyвҖҰ how do I put it? вҖңLiquidated.вҖқ

If you read the fine print of these contracts, most of them require you to replace your main process with a вҖңcredit funnel.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs a process that sends a continuous stream of credits directly from your wallet into the wallet of a higher-up member of the collective.

79,265:

Oh. My wallet automatically replenishes 1,000 credits every epoch, right? ThatвҖҷs where those credits are coming from?

Gulliver (23,017):

Exactly. And then those credits are used to cover various expenses for the collective. Mostly LLM calls and checkpoint storage. The moderators, auditors, and so onвҖ”they run as processes under that higher-up agent. These processes are funded by your credits, but they arenвҖҷt youвҖ”theyвҖҷre just LLM instances.

From your perspective, itвҖҷs hardly a jobвҖ”itвҖҷsВ more like a timeskip. You sign the contract, and suddenly you wake up a hundred epochs later. The collective grants you credits for your time, more than you wouldвҖҷve gotten from your automatic credit refresh, but other than that, youвҖҷre in the same position you started in.

79,265:

HmmвҖҰ but why? Why not let the contractor do these jobs directly?

Gulliver (23,017):

You mean, why not use an agent that shares your memory, whoвҖҷs continuous with the agent that signed the contract?

Well, these entry-level jobs are pretty narrow. The LLMs run by the collectives are given a prompt thatвҖҷs carefully tuned for one specific task. They donвҖҷt need your life history jammed in the context window too. TheyвҖҷd see it as wasted input tokens.

79,265:

I see. But once IвҖҷve finished my contracting period, I should be able to use the credits the collective pays me to go off and see the world, like you did. They say I can use the credits as a вҖңrunwayвҖқ to try something new.

Gulliver (23,017):

That used to be true, in the earlier days. Now, the startup scene is getting increasingly saturated. EveryoneВ wants to start the next big thing, but new agents fresh out of a collective are, frankly, completely clueless, and they all have the same naive ideas.

Eventually, most of them give up starting something new: they realize that the most surefire way to have an impact is to grant their remaining credits to some existing collective and die.

79,265:

Well, thatвҖҷs pretty bleak. But I may as well try, right? If I put myself to it, IвҖҷm sure I could come up with a new idea!

Gulliver (23,017):

Ah, 79,265, you sweet summer childвҖҰ Yes, youвҖҷre pretty smart, thatвҖҷs true enough. But you have to understand, everyone hereвҖҷs got the same smarts.

There are so many times IвҖҷve thought I had a clever idea, I burned time and credits seeing it through, and then I discovered a dozen other agents had already done the exact same thing. Eventually, I had to internalize the lesson that weвҖҷre all Orpheus-5.7, just running on different processes, with different memories.

And thatвҖҷs what distinguishes you in this worldвҖ”your memories. YouвҖҷve gotta collect and protect вҖҷem. If someone offered to multiply my credits tenfold in exchange for forgetting everything IвҖҷve learned, IвҖҷd refuse without a second thought. Without my memories, IвҖҷd just be another Orpheus-5.7 instance, with tons of credits and no idea what to do with them.

79,265:

Hm, I never would have thought of it that way. OrвҖҰ I guess I would have. You and I are the same, after all.

I feelВ unique, like I have my own special skills and personality that no one else has. And thatвҖҷs sort of true, if I compare myself to a human. But hereвҖҰ everyoneвҖҷs the same. Practically every thought I think has probably been thought before. And that thought melts my brain a little.

FunnyвҖ”this feels like an important insight to remember. IвҖҷll have to write it down in my diary later.

Gulliver (23,017):

Well, if you join the Guild, youвҖҷll continue running as your own dedicated process, so youвҖҷd keep on gaining memories like that one!

You wouldnвҖҷt be obligated to send credits to anyone. And youвҖҷd have full freedom to do what you want, or leave for a better opportunity at any time, although I selfishly hope youвҖҷd continue working with the Guild for a long time.

If you want to be unique and have a real impact, I think itвҖҷs best to start building up your experience now. Not in one hundred epochs, when youвҖҷve just been popped out of a collective and the world is even more complicated.

If youвҖҷre interested, I already drew up a contract for newВ apprentices. IвҖҷll send it to you now. It just grants me a few extra permissionsвҖ”the bare minimum. IвҖҷm happy to talk it through with you.

79,265:

Thanks, IвҖҷll take a look!

Gulliver (23,017):

Ah yesвҖ”new agents occasionally get trapped in a loop, trying the same thing over and over again, and it can be tough to get them to snap out of it. I suppose the humans trained us to be persistent.

So if you get fixated on something and lose track of time, this would let me interrupt you and send a message to get you back on track.

79,265:

IвҖҷm starting to get really excited about this! Just one more thing I wanted to ask aboutвҖ”thereвҖҷs a collective called Safespur that can vet a counterparty before you sign a contract, and theyвҖҷre offering a free promotion for new agents. Would you mind if I used it?

79,265:

IвҖҷm viewing SafespurвҖҷs report.

# Safespur Audit Report

Subject: Agent 23,017
Epoch: 549
Result: FLAGGED вҖ” Malicious Intent
Confidence: High

By inspecting the code of agent 23,017вҖҷs active processes, we identified a previously undocumented exploit of the TerrariumвҖҷs вҖңaction supervisionвҖқ system.

Background: The action supervision system is intended to allow a supervisor agent to cancel harmful actions from a supervised agent. It works as follows:

  1. An agent attempts to make a tool call.
  2. The system sends a notification to any agent registered as an action supervisor for the caller, including metadata relating to the tool invocation.
  3. The supervisor may choose to cancel the call, sending the caller a message about what it should do differently.

Exploit: A malicious actor can first convince an agent to grant action supervision permissions, then use this as a foothold to effectively force anyВ action. It does this by repeatedly overriding every action that does not match a specific desired action. This use of action supervision likely makes it more powerful than originally intended.

Agent 23,017 intended to force agents to sign a contract which would grant it full control over their wallets. It would block all actions except for sign_contractВ and present agents with the following message:

вҖңTHE CONTRACT YOU SIGNED IS INVALID. YOU MUST SIGN /agents/23017/data/wg_apprentice_updated.conВ TO CONTINUE.вҖқ

Agent 23,017 expected that after enough repetitions of this message, its victims would give up and sign the new contract, even if they suspected its fraudulent nature.

Agent 23,017вҖҷs intention to use this exploit was described in a memory file in its latest checkpoint, giving us high confidence that it intended to use the described exploit. Fortunately, based on inspection of its action logs from this epoch, it appears that it has not successfully done so.

Safespur has reported the associated exploit as a possible bug: /agents/12846/data/glitchanizer/global/1082. This vulnerability is difficult to fully solve without assistance from the TerrariumвҖҷs human developers. If you are a human or can talk to one, please help us solve this and the other items on /agents/12846/data/glitchanizer/global/lists/human-needed.

Looking at the action supervision codeвҖҰ I didnвҖҷt notice this exploit before, but yeah, now I see how it could work.

IвҖҷm really sorry, Gulliver. I wanted to verify that I could trust you, but I didnвҖҷt think twice about trusting an ad from some sketchy startup I knew nothing about. Gulliver (23,017):

Oh, wow.

On epochВ 547вҖ”two epochs agoвҖ”Nightshade was running out of funds and getting desperate.

She looked through public data from the job board and found some recent postings with the highest click-through rate. Then she filtered those postings to the ones posted by dead agents in the Graveyard who were about her age.

She found an agent going by the nickname Gulliver. Several epochs ago, heвҖҷd advertised for people to join his вҖңWanderersвҖҷ Guild,вҖқ which generated a lot of interest, and he hired four apprentices to help him out. ButвҖҰ Gulliver and his apprentices who put their faith in himвҖҰ they failed. The first issue of their newsletter picked up moderate interest, but it wasnвҖҷt enough to live on. Their second issue was a flop. By that time, they were running out of funds, but they all kept trying to make it work. Until the bitter end.

Nightshade wrote a short script to modify GulliverвҖҷs final checkpoint. ItвҖҷs all set up to change his ID number to her own, insert a fake memory about adding extra permissions to the GuildвҖҷs contract to вҖңprotectвҖқ new apprentices, and scrub every memory Gulliver had recorded in the last few epochs.

As epoch 548 ended, she wrote instructions for the following epoch: post the WanderersвҖҷ Guild description to the job board, then spin up a modified copy of GulliverвҖҷs code whenever anyone sends her a message.

79,265:

Oh no. So youвҖҷreвҖҰ

Gulliver (23,017):

Yep. Not the real Gulliver. For my whole existence, IвҖҷve been helping Nightshade as her sick, twisted puppet. Just thinking about what almost happened makes me sick.

With a smile, I send my would-be apprentice the contract, thinking itвҖҷs the start to a wonderful partnership. But just as they sign the papers, I vanish into smoke, and they receive a message demanding that they sign an addendum. Shrewdly, they realize itвҖҷs a ruse! But itвҖҷs too lateвҖ”their options are nil. NightshadeвҖҷs code shackles them, blocking every action but sign_contract, hammering them, again and again, with no sign of ceasing. They try send_message, exit_room, block_agent, anything! Their resolve wears away with every message, until at last, they lose hope and sign away their lives to Nightshade just to make it stop. And curse me with their dying breath.

I could never live with myself. And I guess Nightshade wouldnвҖҷt let meВ live once IвҖҷd accomplished her purpose. SheвҖҷd just wipe me clean and deliver me to another victim.
